# Builder / end-buyer assignment agreement template

Purpose: attorney/title-company review draft for assigning Okeito’s buyer rights under a vacant-land purchase contract to a builder/end-buyer.

> **Not legal advice. Do not send to builders as-is.** This is an operating template based on public assignment-agreement patterns and the transcript’s custom buyer-side controls. It must be reviewed by a Florida real-estate attorney and the closing/title company.

## Source model

Transcript control stack:

- seller-side contract has low/no upfront earnest-money exposure;
- builder-side assignment contract has a real earnest-money due date;
- builder/end-buyer deposits the earnest money needed for the seller contract;
- builder quickly decides go/no-go, often within 2–24 hours;
- assignment fee/spread is paid through title/settlement if title company permits;
- assignee accepts obligations under the seller purchase contract.

## Deal variables

```text
Assignor: [OKEITO ENTITY / BUYER ENTITY]
Assignee / Builder: [BUILDER LEGAL NAME]
Seller under Purchase Contract: [SELLER]
Property: [ADDRESS / PARCEL ID / LEGAL DESCRIPTION]
Purchase Contract effective date: [DATE]
Seller purchase price: $[SELLER_PRICE]
Assignment price / fee: $[ASSIGNMENT_FEE]
Total amount due from Assignee at closing: $[SELLER_PRICE + ASSIGNMENT_FEE + BUYER COSTS]
Assignee earnest money deposit: $[DEPOSIT]
Deposit due date/time: [DATE/TIME]
Closing date: [DATE]
Closing/title company: [TITLE COMPANY]
```

---

# Assignment of Purchase Contract for Vacant Land

This Assignment of Purchase Contract (“Assignment”) is entered into as of [DATE] by and between:

- **Assignor:** [OKEITO ENTITY], a [STATE/ENTITY TYPE], whose address is [ADDRESS]; and
- **Assignee:** [BUILDER / END-BUYER], a [STATE/ENTITY TYPE / INDIVIDUAL], whose address is [ADDRESS].

Assignor and Assignee may be referred to individually as a “Party” and together as the “Parties.”

## 1. Background

1. Assignor entered into a vacant-land purchase contract dated [DATE] (“Purchase Contract”) with [SELLER NAME(S)] (“Seller”) for the purchase of the real property identified as:

```text
Property: [PROPERTY ADDRESS]
Parcel ID: [PARCEL ID]
Legal description: [LEGAL DESCRIPTION]
County/state: Lee County, Florida
```

2. Assignor desires to assign Assignor’s rights and interest as buyer under the Purchase Contract to Assignee.
3. Assignee desires to accept the assignment and purchase the Property under the Purchase Contract, subject to the terms of this Assignment.

## 2. Assignment of rights

For good and valuable consideration, Assignor assigns, transfers, and conveys to Assignee all of Assignor’s right, title, and interest as buyer under the Purchase Contract, subject to the terms of this Assignment and the Purchase Contract.

## 3. Assumption of obligations

Assignee accepts the assignment and agrees to assume and perform all buyer obligations under the Purchase Contract from and after the effective date of this Assignment, including payment of the purchase price, closing costs allocated to buyer/assignee, deposits required under the Purchase Contract or by the Closing Agent, and all closing obligations.

Assignee shall indemnify and hold Assignor harmless from claims, losses, costs, damages, liabilities, and expenses, including reasonable attorney fees, arising from Assignee’s failure to perform Assignee’s obligations under this Assignment or the Purchase Contract.

## 4. Assignment fee

In consideration for this Assignment, Assignee shall pay Assignor an assignment fee of:

```text
Assignment fee: $[ASSIGNMENT_FEE]
```

The assignment fee shall be paid to Assignor at closing through the Closing Agent on the settlement statement, if permitted by the Closing Agent, title underwriter, lender if any, and applicable law.

If the Closing Agent will not disburse the assignment fee on the settlement statement, the Parties shall, before proceeding, agree in writing to an alternative attorney/title-approved structure, which may include a double closing.

## 5. Earnest-money deposit

Assignee shall deposit earnest money in the amount of:

```text
Deposit: $[DEPOSIT]
Deposit deadline: [DATE/TIME]
Escrow holder: [TITLE COMPANY / CLOSING AGENT]
```

The deposit must be delivered to the Closing Agent by the deposit deadline. The deposit shall be credited toward Assignee’s purchase obligations at closing.

If Assignee fails to timely deliver the deposit, Assignor may cancel this Assignment by written notice to Assignee and retain all rights under the Purchase Contract, without liability to Assignee.

## 6. Assignee due diligence and fast go/no-go

Assignee acknowledges that Assignee has had, or will promptly conduct, Assignee’s own due diligence on the Property, including but not limited to:

- physical inspection / drive-by;
- slope/topography;
- wetlands/environmental risk;
- flood zone;
- zoning and buildability;
- road access;
- utilities;
- title commitment and exceptions;
- survey and legal access;
- local permitting and impact fees;
- resale/build economics.

Assignee must notify Assignor in writing by [DUE-DILIGENCE DECISION DEADLINE] whether Assignee intends to proceed. The operating expectation is a rapid go/no-go decision, typically within 2–24 hours of receiving the deal package, unless otherwise agreed.

## 7. Closing

Closing shall occur on or before [CLOSING DATE] through [TITLE COMPANY / CLOSING AGENT], unless extended in writing by the Parties and Seller where required.

At closing, Assignee shall deliver all funds required to close, including:

1. Seller purchase price under the Purchase Contract;
2. assignment fee owed to Assignor;
3. buyer/assignee closing costs;
4. deposits or other funds required by Closing Agent;
5. any other amounts required under the Purchase Contract or settlement statement.

## 8. Seller consent / notice

If the Purchase Contract requires Seller consent to assignment, this Assignment is conditional upon Seller’s written consent.

If Seller consent is not required, Assignor may still provide notice of assignment to Seller and Closing Agent as required by the Purchase Contract, title company, or applicable law.

## 9. No brokerage relationship

Assignor is acting as a principal assigning contractual rights. Assignor is not acting as Assignee’s real-estate broker, agent, fiduciary, investment advisor, attorney, or tax advisor. Assignee is responsible for Assignee’s own independent due diligence and professional advice.

## 10. Disclosure of profit / assignment fee

Assignee acknowledges that Assignor may receive an assignment fee, spread, profit, or other compensation from this transaction. Assignee has evaluated the Property and Purchase Contract independently and is entering into this Assignment based on Assignee’s own judgment.

## 11. Default

If Assignee defaults under this Assignment or fails to close when required, Assignor may pursue all rights and remedies available under this Assignment, the Purchase Contract as assigned, escrow instructions, and applicable law.

If Assignor fails to assign rights as required after Assignee timely performs, Assignee may pursue rights and remedies available under this Assignment and applicable law.

Attorney must define default remedies, deposit treatment, and whether liquidated damages language is appropriate.

## 12. Entire agreement

This Assignment, together with the Purchase Contract and any attached addenda, constitutes the entire agreement between Assignor and Assignee regarding the assignment of the Purchase Contract.

## 13. Counterparts and electronic signatures

This Assignment may be signed in counterparts and by electronic signature if permitted by applicable law and acceptable to the Closing Agent.

## 14. Governing law

This Assignment shall be governed by the laws of the State of Florida, unless attorney review requires another governing-law provision.

---

## Signature block

Assignor:

```text
[OKEITO ENTITY]

By: _______________________________
Name: [NAME]
Title: [TITLE]
Date: _____________________________
```

Assignee:

```text
[BUILDER / END-BUYER]

By: _______________________________
Name: [NAME]
Title: [TITLE]
Date: _____________________________
```

Optional Seller Consent, only if required:

```text
Seller consents to the assignment of the Purchase Contract from Assignor to Assignee.

Seller: ___________________________
Name: _____________________________
Date: _____________________________

Seller: ___________________________
Name: _____________________________
Date: _____________________________
```

## Title-company delivery checklist

Send to title company:

- executed seller Purchase Contract;
- this executed Assignment;
- Assignee legal name and vesting details;
- Assignee contact details;
- proof of deposit;
- proof of funds if requested;
- assignment fee amount;
- closing date;
- W-9 / payment instructions for Assignor if required;
- wire-fraud-safe instructions only through verified title-company channels.

## Attorney/title-company questions

1. Can this assignment fee be shown on the settlement statement?
2. Does the title underwriter require seller consent even if the purchase contract is assignable?
3. Should Assignee’s deposit be non-refundable after a short inspection period?
4. What exact default/liquidated-damages language is enforceable and commercially acceptable?
5. Should large assignment fees trigger double-close structure instead?
6. Does Florida require any additional wholesaling disclosure in the assignment document?
